-- 1 of 6 --

The 5-Step Pre-VSL Checklist
1. Customer Survey
Use Survey Monkey or any online survey tool to survey your prospective
buyers. Ask the following 5 questions:
— What is your most pressing concern when it comes to (GOAL)?
— What’s the #1 thing you want to know about (GOAL)?
— What word or phrase would inspire you to buy a book about (GOAL) if you saw that word or
phrase on the cover?
— What would you have to see to know you achieved your (GOAL) objectives?
— What would if feel like to achieve your (GOAL) objectives?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

The 5-Step Pre-VSL Checklist
2. Top 3 Competitor’s Websites
You want to analyze your to 3 competitor’s websites (VSLs or sales pages.)
Avoid plagiarizing at all costs. You are looking for key words and phrases
that are consistent in the sales copy.
Analyze the pages in 3 steps:
1. Transcribe: Hire a transcriptionist on fiver.com to transcribe the VSL. If it’s
a written sales page, simply copy it into Word.
2. Word Cloud: Google “Word Cloud” and find a free word cloud site. Paste
the sales copy for each page into the site. Look for top 5 words.
3. Hand-write: Write the sales copy by hand for 1, 2 or all 3 sites.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

The 5-Step Pre-VSL Checklist
3. Write 10 Headlines
Sometimes VSLs have no headlines. Most of the time, they have one. John
Carlton, one of the world’s best copywriters, would write 200 headlines for a
sales page. I suggest you start with 10 headlines. Use my Headline
Formula Subject Lines as a starting place.
Then…
1. Survey: Ask your potential customers to rank their interest “in reading
more about X” given the subject line.
2. Traffic Test: Spend $50 and test each subject line as a PPC or banner ad
on Google or Facebook. The most clicks wins.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

The 5-Step Pre-VSL Checklist
4. Survey Price Points
Write a synopsis of your product or service (use Sellerator’s
Persuasion Formula tutorials) and conduct another survey. This time,
you want to ask your potential customers how much they would pay
for a “straight to-the-point how-to blueprint to achieving (GOAL)”. If
you know your bonuses, include a short description of them.
BEST APPROACH: Leave the field blank and let the user name the
price.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 6 --

The 5-Step Pre-VSL Checklist
5. Brainstorm Bullets & Benefits
While this step is not necessary, you’ll find it incredibly helpful.
Using the Sellerator notes feature, or any text editing document, set
a timer for 10 minutes. Clear yourself of all distractions. Do nothing
for 10 minutes but write down one benefit after another that your
product or service provides.
Then, after the 10 minutes is over, turn each of these into a bullet by
using my Super Bullet Trick.
Example: 	“Simple” becomes “Deceptively Simple!”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
